Day 14 - ThreadX¶

Overview¶

Day 14 - ThreadX¶

Focus¶

This day concentrates on deterministic ThreadX primitives and certification-friendly patterns.

Learning Objectives¶

  • Understand the core concept and where it appears in embedded products.

  • Build or reason about one practical implementation pattern.

  • Identify failure modes and prevention checks for production firmware.

Deliverables¶

  • Concept notes in lesson.rst.

  • Practice problems in exercises.rst.

  • Guided answer strategy in solutions.rst.

Definition of Done¶

  • You can explain the concept without notes.

  • You can map the concept to at least one real product scenario.

  • You can list at least three verification checks for correctness.

Lesson¶

Day 14 Lesson - ThreadX (Azure RTOS)¶

ThreadX (now Azure RTOS ThreadX) is Microsoft’s industrial-grade RTOS with extensive certifications.

Key Features: - Safety-certified (IEC 61508, DO-178B, etc.) - Deterministic performance - Priority inheritance - Thread time-slicing - Azure cloud integration

API Style: - tx_thread_create() - tx_semaphore_get() - tx_queue_send() - tx_mutex_put()

When to use: Safety-critical applications, medical devices, Azure IoT integration.

Exercises¶

Day 14 Exercises - ThreadX¶

Exercise 1: Safety-critical controller with ThreadX. Exercise 2: Azure IoT device telemetry with ThreadX. Exercise 3: Real-time performance analysis and certification prep.

Solutions¶

Day 14 Solutions - ThreadX¶

Complete ThreadX implementations with safety patterns and Azure integration examples.

  • Next (15)
